name: Robert Graettinger
abstract: Robert Frederick Graettinger (October 31, 1923 – March 12, 1957) was an American composer, best known for his work with Stan Kenton.

about_quotes.speaker: Holman Bill
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] He had quite a stable of arrangers on this date. He had Johnny Richards, Pete Rugalo, Gene Roland, Bob Graettinger. So already quite a stable of arrangers. [INTERVIEWEE] Yeah. Those were guys who had been writing for him.
about_quotes.doc_title: Holman Bill — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Konitz Lee
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] ...gave a lot of good writers their break like Holman or Lenny Niehaus, or Rugolo, or Graettinger... [INTERVIEWEE] I had some kids by then... I was with that band for about a year and a half...
about_quotes.doc_title: Konitz Lee — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: J. Leo Rayhill
about_quotes.evidence: He got people like Bob Graettinger, who is a genius, absolute genius, but I couldn’t play it on the show
about_quotes.doc_title: J. Leo Rayhill interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, March 23, 2007
